children.heading: Summary plan description and information to be furnished to participants and beneficiaries
children.label: a Summary plan description and information to be furnished to participants and beneficiaries

children.text: a summary plan description described in section 1022(a)(1) of this title; and1See References in Text note below.1
the information described in subsection (f) and sections 1024(b)(3) and 1025(a) and (c) of this title.

children.heading: Reports to be filed with Secretary of Labor
children.label: b Reports to be filed with Secretary of Labor

children.text: the annual report containing the information required by ; andsection 1023 of this title
terminal and supplementary reports as required by subsection (c) of this section.

Each administrator of an employee pension benefit plan which is winding up its affairs (without regard to the number of participants remaining in the plan) shall, in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary, file such terminal reports as the Secretary may consider necessary. A copy of such report shall also be filed with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation.
The Secretary may require terminal reports to be filed with regard to any employee welfare benefit plan which is winding up its affairs in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Secretary.

The Secretary may require that a plan described in paragraph (1) or (2) file a supplementary or terminal report with the annual report in the year such plan is terminated and that a copy of such supplementary or terminal report in the case of a plan described in paragraph (1) be also filed with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation.

children.heading: Notice of failure to meet minimum funding standards
children.label: d Notice of failure to meet minimum funding standards

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If an employer maintaining a plan other than a multiemployer plan fails to make a required installment or other payment required to meet the minimum funding standard under to a plan before the 60th day following the due date for such installment or other payment, the employer shall notify each participant and beneficiary (including an alternate payee as defined in ) of such plan of such failure. Such notice shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe.section 1082 of this titlesection 1056(d)(3)(K) of this title

children.heading: Subsection not to apply if waiver pending
children.label: 2 Subsection not to apply if waiver pending
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any failure if the employer has filed a waiver request under section 1083 or 1085a of this title with respect to the plan year to which the required installment relates, except that if the waiver request is denied, notice under paragraph (1) shall be provided within 60 days after the date of such denial.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 3 Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the terms “required installment” and “due date” have the same meanings given such terms by section 1083(j) or 1085a(f) of this title, whichever is applicable.

children.heading: Notice of transfer of excess pension assets to health benefits accounts
children.label: e Notice of transfer of excess pension assets to health benefits accounts

children.heading: Notice to participants
children.label: 1 Notice to participants
children.text: Not later than 60 days before the date of a qualified transfer by an employee pension benefit plan of excess pension assets to a health benefits account or applicable life insurance account, the administrator of the plan shall notify (in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe) each participant and beneficiary under the plan of such transfer. Such notice shall include information with respect to the amount of excess pension assets, the portion to be transferred, the amount of health benefits liabilities or applicable life insurance benefit liabilities expected to be provided with the assets transferred, and the amount of pension benefits of the participant which will be nonforfeitable immediately after the transfer.

children.heading: Notice to Secretaries, administrator, and employee organizations
children.label: 2 Notice to Secretaries, administrator, and employee organizations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days before the date of any qualified transfer by an employee pension benefit plan of excess pension assets to a health benefits account or applicable life insurance account, the employer maintaining the plan from which the transfer is made shall provide the Secretary, the Secretary of the Treasury, the administrator, and each employee organization representing participants in the plan a written notice of such transfer. A copy of any such notice shall be available for inspection in the principal office of the administrator.

children.heading: Information relating to transfer
children.label: B Information relating to transfer
children.text: Such notice shall identify the plan from which the transfer is made, the amount of the transfer, a detailed accounting of assets projected to be held by the plan immediately before and immediately after the transfer, and the current liabilities under the plan at the time of the transfer.

children.heading: Authority for additional reporting requirements
children.label: C Authority for additional reporting requirements
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe such additional reporting requirements as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 3 Definitions
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), any term used in such paragraph which is also used in (as in effect on ) shall have the same meaning as when used in such section.section 420 of title 26December 29, 2022

children.heading: Defined benefit plan funding notices
children.label: f Defined benefit plan funding notices

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The administrator of a defined benefit plan to which subchapter III applies shall for each plan year provide a plan funding notice to the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, to each plan participant and beneficiary, to each labor organization representing such participants or beneficiaries, and, in the case of a multiemployer plan, to each employer that has an obligation to contribute to the plan.

children.heading: Identifying information
children.label: A Identifying information
children.text: Each notice required under paragraph (1) shall contain identifying information, including the name of the plan, the address and phone number of the plan administrator and the plan’s principal administrative officer, each plan sponsor’s employer identification number, and the plan number of the plan.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a single-employer plan, a statement as to whether the plan’s percentage of plan liabilities funded (as described in clause (ii)(I)(bb)) for the plan year to which the notice relates, and for the 2 preceding plan years, is at least 100 percent (and, if not, the actual percentages), or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, a statement as to whether the plan’s funded percentage (as defined in section 1085(i) of this title) for the plan year to which the notice relates, and for the 2 preceding plan years, is at least 100 percent (and, if not, the actual percentages),1

children.label: aa
children.text: a statement of the value of the plan’s assets and liabilities for the plan year to which the notice relates as of the last day of the plan year to which the notice relates, and for the preceding 2 plan years as of the last day of each such plan year, determined using the asset valuation under subclause (II) of and the interest rate under ,section 1306(a)(3)(E)(iii) of this titlesection 1306(a)(3)(E)(iv) of this title

children.label: bb
children.text: for purposes of the statement in subparagraph (B)(i)(I), the percentage of plan liabilities funded, calculated as the ratio between the value of the plan’s assets and liabilities, as determined under item (aa), for the plan year to which the notice relates and for the 2 preceding plan years, and

children.label: cc
children.text: if the information in (aa) and (bb) is presented in tabular form, a statement that describes that in the event of a plan termination the corporation’s calculation of plan liabilities may be greater and that references the section of the notice with the information required under clause (x), and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, a statement, for the plan year to which the notice relates and the preceding 2 plan years, of the value of the plan assets (determined both in the same manner as under and under the rules of subclause (I)(aa)) and the value of the plan liabilities (determined in the same manner as under except that the method specified in section 1085(i)(8) of this title shall be used),section 1084 of this titlesection 1084 of this title1

children.label: I
children.text: retired or separated from service and are receiving benefits,

children.label: II
children.text: retired or separated participants entitled to future benefits, and

children.label: III
children.text: active participants under the plan,

children.label: iv
children.text: a statement setting forth the funding policy of the plan, the asset allocation of investments under the plan (expressed as percentages of total assets), and the average return on assets for the plan year, as of the end of the plan year to which the notice relates,

children.label: I
children.text: a statement describing how a person may obtain a copy of the plan’s funding improvement or rehabilitation plan, as appropriate, adopted under and the actuarial and financial data that demonstrate any action taken by the plan toward fiscal improvement, andsection 1085 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: a summary of any funding improvement plan, rehabilitation plan, or modification thereof adopted under during the plan year to which the notice relates,section 1085 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the projected date of insolvency;

children.label: II
children.text: a clear statement that such insolvency may result in benefit reductions; and

children.label: III
children.text: a statement describing whether the plan sponsor has taken legally permitted actions to prevent insolvency.2So in original. The period probably should be a comma.2

children.label: vii
children.text: in the case of any plan amendment, scheduled benefit increase or reduction, or other known event taking effect in the current plan year and having a material effect on plan liabilities or assets for the year (as defined in regulations by the Secretary), an explanation of the amendment, schedule increase or reduction, or event, and a projection to the end of such plan year of the effect of the amendment, scheduled increase or reduction, or event on plan liabilities,

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a single-employer plan, a summary of the rules governing termination of single-employer plans under subtitle C of subchapter III, or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, a summary of the rules governing reorganization or insolvency, including the limitations on benefit payments,

children.label: I
children.text: the plan’s assets, as of the last day of the plan year and for the 2 preceding plan years, as determined under clause (ii)(I)(aa),

children.label: II
children.text: the plan’s liabilities, as of the last day of the plan year and for the 2 preceding plan years, as determined under clause (ii)(1)(aa), and

children.label: III
children.text: the funded status of the plan, determined as the ratio of the plan’s assets and liabilities calculated under subclauses (I) and (II), for the plan year to which the notice relates, and for the 2 preceding plan years,

children.label: I
children.text: if plan assets are determined to be sufficient to pay vested benefits that are not guaranteed by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, participants and beneficiaries may receive benefits in excess of the guaranteed amount, and

children.label: II
children.text: such a determination generally uses assumptions that result in a plan having a lower funded status as compared to the plan’s funded status disclosed in this notice.

children.label: xi
children.text: a statement that a person may obtain a copy of the annual report of the plan filed under upon request, through the Internet website of the Department of Labor, or through an Intranet website maintained by the applicable plan sponsor (or plan administrator on behalf of the plan sponsor), andsection 1024(a) of this title

children.label: xii
children.text: if applicable, a statement that each contributing sponsor, and each member of the contributing sponsor’s controlled group, of the single-employer plan was required to provide the information under for the plan year to which the notice relates.section 1310 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, a statement that the plan administrator shall provide, upon written request, to any labor organization representing plan participants and beneficiaries and any employer that has an obligation to contribute to the plan, a copy of the annual report filed with the Secretary under , andsection 1024(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: any additional information which the plan administrator elects to include to the extent not inconsistent with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Effect of segment rate stabilization on plan funding
children.label: D Effect of segment rate stabilization on plan funding
I
children.text: a statement that the MAP-21, the Highway and Transportation Funding Act of 2014,, the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015,, the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021, and the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act modified the method for determining the interest rates used to determine the actuarial value of benefits earned under the plan, providing for a 25-year average of interest rates to be taken into account in addition to a 2-year average,33So in original.3

children.label: II
children.text: a statement that, as a result of the MAP-21, the Highway and Transportation Funding Act of 2014,, the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015,, the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021, and the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, the plan sponsor may contribute less money to the plan when interest rates are at historical lows, and33

children.label: III
children.text: a table which shows (determined both with and without regard to ) the funding target attainment percentage (as defined in ), the funding shortfall (as defined in ), and the minimum required contribution (as determined under ), for the applicable plan year and each of the 2 preceding plan years.section 1083(h)(2)(C)(iv) of this titlesection 1083(d)(2) of this titlesection 1083(c)(4) of this titlesection 1083 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the funding target (as defined in ) is less than 95 percent of such funding target determined without regard to ,section 1083(d)(2) of this titlesection 1083(h)(2)(C)(iv) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the plan has a funding shortfall (as defined in and determined without regard to ) greater than $500,000, andsection 1083(c)(4) of this titlesection 1083(h)(2)(C)(iv) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: the plan had 50 or more participants on any day during the preceding plan year.

children.heading: Special rule for plan years beginning before 2012
children.label: iii Special rule for plan years beginning before 2012
children.text: In the case of a preceding plan year referred to in clause (i)(III) which begins before , the information described in such clause shall be provided only without regard to .January 1, 2012section 1083(h)(2)(C)(iv) of this title

children.heading: Effect of CSEC plan rules on plan funding
children.label: E Effect of CSEC plan rules on plan funding
i
children.text: a statement that different rules apply to CSEC plans than apply to single-employer plans,

children.label: ii
children.text: for the first 2 plan years beginning after , a statement that, as a result of changes in the law made by the Cooperative and Small Employer Charity Pension Flexibility Act, the contributions to the plan may have changed, andDecember 31, 2013

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a CSEC plan that is in funding restoration status for the plan year, a statement that the plan is in funding restoration status for such plan year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any notice under paragraph (1) shall be provided not later than 120 days after the end of the plan year to which the notice relates.

children.heading: Exception for small plans
children.label: B Exception for small plans
children.text: In the case of a small plan (as such term is used under ) any notice under paragraph (1) shall be provided upon filing of the annual report under .section 1083(g)(2)(B) of this titlesection 1024(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall be provided in a form and manner prescribed in regulations of the Secretary,

children.label: B
children.text: shall be written in a manner so as to be understood by the average plan participant, and

children.label: C
children.text: may be provided in written, electronic, or other appropriate form to the extent such form is reasonably accessible to persons to whom the notice is required to be provided.

children.heading: Reporting by certain arrangements
children.label: g Reporting by certain arrangements
children.text: The Secretary shall, by regulation, require multiple employer welfare arrangements providing benefits consisting of medical care (within the meaning of ) which are not group health plans to register with the Secretary prior to operating in a State and may, by regulation, require such multiple employer welfare arrangements to report, not more frequently than annually, in such form and such manner as the Secretary may require for the purpose of determining the extent to which the requirements of part 7 are being carried out in connection with such benefits.section 1191b(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: No employer reports
children.label: 1 No employer reports
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, no report shall be required under this section by an employer maintaining a qualified salary reduction arrangement under .section 408(p) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: The name and address of the employer and the trustee.

children.label: B
children.text: The requirements for eligibility for participation.

children.label: C
children.text: The benefits provided with respect to the arrangement.

children.label: D
children.text: The time and method of making elections with respect to the arrangement.

children.label: E
children.text: The procedures for, and effects of, withdrawals (including rollovers) from the arrangement.

children.heading: Employee notification
children.label: 3 Employee notification
children.text: The employer shall notify each employee immediately before the period for which an election described in may be made of the employee’s opportunity to make such election. Such notice shall include a copy of the description described in paragraph (2).section 408(p)(5)(C) of title 26

children.heading: Notice of blackout periods to participant or beneficiary under individual account plan
children.label: i Notice of blackout periods to participant or beneficiary under individual account plan

children.heading: Duties of plan administrator
children.label: 1 Duties of plan administrator
children.text: In advance of the commencement of any blackout period with respect to an individual account plan, the plan administrator shall notify the plan participants and beneficiaries who are affected by such action in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: the reasons for the blackout period,

children.label: ii
children.text: an identification of the investments and other rights affected,

children.label: iii
children.text: the expected beginning date and length of the blackout period,

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of investments affected, a statement that the participant or beneficiary should evaluate the appropriateness of their current investment decisions in light of their inability to direct or diversify assets credited to their accounts during the blackout period, and

children.label: v
children.text: such other matters as the Secretary may require by regulation.

children.heading: Notice to participants and beneficiaries
children.label: B Notice to participants and beneficiaries
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, notices described in paragraph (1) shall be furnished to all participants and beneficiaries under the plan to whom the blackout period applies at least 30 days in advance of the blackout period.

children.label: i
children.text: a deferral of the blackout period would violate the requirements of subparagraph (A) or (B) of , and a fiduciary of the plan reasonably so determines in writing, orsection 1104(a)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the inability to provide the 30-day advance notice is due to events that were unforeseeable or circumstances beyond the reasonable control of the plan administrator, and a fiduciary of the plan reasonably so determines in writing,

children.heading: Written notice
children.label: D Written notice
children.text: The notice required to be provided under this subsection shall be in writing, except that such notice may be in electronic or other form to the extent that such form is reasonably accessible to the recipient.

children.heading: Notice to issuers of employer securities subject to blackout period
children.label: E Notice to issuers of employer securities subject to blackout period
children.text: In the case of any blackout period in connection with an individual account plan, the plan administrator shall provide timely notice of such blackout period to the issuer of any employer securities subject to such blackout period.

children.heading: Exception for blackout periods with limited applicability
children.label: 3 Exception for blackout periods with limited applicability
children.text: In any case in which the blackout period applies only to 1 or more participants or beneficiaries in connection with a merger, acquisition, divestiture, or similar transaction involving the plan or plan sponsor and occurs solely in connection with becoming or ceasing to be a participant or beneficiary under the plan by reason of such merger, acquisition, divestiture, or transaction, the requirement of this subsection that the notice be provided to all participants and beneficiaries shall be treated as met if the notice required under paragraph (1) is provided to such participants or beneficiaries to whom the blackout period applies as soon as reasonably practicable.

children.heading: Changes in length of blackout period
children.label: 4 Changes in length of blackout period
children.text: If, following the furnishing of the notice pursuant to this subsection, there is a change in the beginning date or length of the blackout period (specified in such notice pursuant to paragraph (2)(A)(iii)), the administrator shall provide affected participants and beneficiaries notice of the change as soon as reasonably practicable. In relation to the extended blackout period, such notice shall meet the requirements of paragraph (2)(D) and shall specify any material change in the matters referred to in clauses (i) through (v) of paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Regulatory exceptions
children.label: 5 Regulatory exceptions
children.text: The Secretary may provide by regulation for additional exceptions to the requirements of this subsection which the Secretary determines are in the interests of participants and beneficiaries.

children.heading: Guidance and model notices
children.label: 6 Guidance and model notices
children.text: The Secretary shall issue guidance and model notices which meet the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “blackout period” means, in connection with an individual account plan, any period for which any ability of participants or beneficiaries under the plan, which is otherwise available under the terms of such plan, to direct or diversify assets credited to their accounts, to obtain loans from the plan, or to obtain distributions from the plan is temporarily suspended, limited, or restricted, if such suspension, limitation, or restriction is for any period of more than 3 consecutive business days.

children.label: ii

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: 3 Limitations
children.text: In no case shall a participant, beneficiary, employee representative, or employer be entitled under this subsection to receive more than one copy of any document described in paragraph (1) during any one 12-month period, or, in the case of any document described in subparagraph (E), (F), (G), (H) or (I) of paragraph (1), a copy of any such document that as of the date on which the request is received by the administrator, has been in the administrator’s possession for 6 years or more. If the administrator provides a copy of a document described in paragraph (1) to any person upon request, the administrator shall be considered as having met any obligation the administrator may have under any other provision of this subchapter to furnish a copy of the same document to such person upon request. The administrator may make a reasonable charge to cover copying, mailing, and other costs of furnishing copies of information pursuant to paragraph (1). The Secretary may by regulations prescribe the maximum amount which will constitute a reasonable charge under the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: 3 Limitations
children.text: In no case shall an employer be entitled under this subsection to receive more than one notice described in paragraph (1) during any one 12-month period. The person required to provide such notice may make a reasonable charge to cover copying, mailing, and other costs of furnishing such notice pursuant to paragraph (1). The Secretary may by regulations prescribe the maximum amount which will constitute a reasonable charge under the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Pension-linked emergency savings accounts
children.label: n Pension-linked emergency savings accounts
children.text: Nothing in this section shall preclude the Secretary from providing, by regulations or otherwise, simplified reporting procedures or requirements regarding such a pension-linked emergency savings account.
